name: Red-tailed Hawk
ecosystem: Open country. Fields, meadows, agricultural land, highway medians, suburban parks, forest edges. Most broadly distributed raptor in North America.
conservation: Least Concern (IUCN). ~2 million US population. Most abundant diurnal raptor in North America. DDT-recovered. Protected by Migratory Bird Treaty Act.

vocalization.description: Ragged, raspy, descending: keeeeeee-arrr. Approximately 2-3 seconds. Broad-spectrum frequency for open-country carrying power.
vocalization.function: Territorial advertisement, intruder alarm, mate communication, courtship display
vocalization.notable: THE HOLLYWOOD HAWK CALL. Used in cinema for Bald Eagles, Golden Eagles, Ospreys, Peregrine Falcons, Tolkien's Great Eagles, and every other raptor regardless of species. Cross-reference degree 221: Bald Eagle's actual call is thin kee-kee-kee; Red-tail's scream replaced it.

genus_transition.ecological_shift: Forest interior pursuit hunters (accipiters) to open-country soaring hunters (buteos). Short wings to broad wings. Dense forest to highway median.

ecology.habitat: Open country, agricultural fields, highway corridors, suburban parks, forest edges. Most ecologically flexible raptor in North America.
ecology.highway_connection: Interstate highway system created millions of acres of optimal hawk habitat in grassy medians. Red-tail is inadvertent beneficiary.
ecology.prey: Generalist: voles, mice, ground squirrels, rabbits, snakes, birds opportunistically
ecology.plumage: Variable: light, dark, intermediate morphs. B. j. calurus (western PNW subspecies) includes all morphs. Red tail diagnostic for adults.

connections.description: Gas Huffer (E=7). Both are sources of borrowed energy. Red-tail scream used for all raptors in cinema; punk genre's energy borrowed/processed by commercial grunge.
Bald Eagle contact call (thin kee-kee-kee). Arc from 221 to 228 = seven-degree correction of most widespread wildlife sound misattribution in popular culture.
Northern Goshawk opened accipiter trio. Red-tail closes entire raptor arc 221-228 as first Buteo.
Sharp-shinned Hawk (final Accipiter). 227-228 boundary = Accipiter-to-Buteo transition. Forest interior to open country.
